title: Device for aim training
abstract: Disclosed is a device for aim training allowing users of the device to practice their aim by shooting the moving targets. The device is rotated by a geared motor and plurality of bevel gears mounted over the shaft. The bevel gears are configured to rotate the targets in predefined direction. The device is supported with rotating wheel that travel around the specified path. The device is configured with an external casing to protect the device from external damage.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A device for aim training 100 allowing the users to practice their aim characterized in that having a geared motor 110, a plurality of support wheels 142, 142a, 142b, 142c, a plurality of support frames 150, a wheel track 146 in accordance with the present invention, said device 100 comprising: a large bevel gear 138 mounted on a primary shaft 114, the primary shaft 114 affixed within the geared motor 110, a plurality of top plates 118, 118a, 118b, 118c connecting with the primary shaft 114; the plurality of top plates 118, 118a, 118b, 118c having a respective plurality of top frame support structure 152, 152a, 152b, 152c, a plurality of vertical bevel gears 135 and a plurality of horizontal bevel gears 134 respectively; and a plurality of horizontal shaft 130 connecting the horizontal bevel gears 134, the horizontal bevel gears 134 interlocking with the large bevel gear 138, a plurality of bevel alignment plates 166 mounting the vertical bevel gears 135 over the respective top plates 152, 152a, 152b, 152c, a plurality of secondary shafts 126 mounted over the top plates 118, 118a, 118b, 118c having its one end connected with the vertical bevel gears 135 respectively, each plurality of secondary shaft 126 having a plurality of targets 122 mounted over the plurality of support frames 150 respectively. 2. The device for aiming training 100 as claimed in claim 1, wherein the support wheels 142, 142a, 142b, 142c connected with the top frame support structure 152, 152a, 152b, 152c respectively. 3. The device for aiming training 100 as claimed in claim 1, wherein the support wheels 142, 142a, 142b, 142c run over the wheel track 146 in a clockwise direction. 4. The device for aiming training 100 as claimed in claim 1, wherein the horizontal bevel gears 134 and the vertical bevel gears 135 rotate the plurality of secondary shaft 126 in an anticlockwise direction. 5. The device for aiming training 100 as claimed in claim 1, wherein the horizontal bevel gears 134 are positioned at a 90Â° angle over the large bevel gear 138.
